did.unittitle: Leonard Baskin papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 171-175 (Esther Baskin papers): Restricted until April 22, 2026. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Leonard Baskin Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Leonard Baskin Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven groupings: December 1964 acquisitions; April 1991 acquisition; 8 September 1994 acquisition; 9 September 1994 acquisition; September 1995 acquisition; September 1998 acquisition; December 2011 acquisitions.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.
Former call numbers: Uncat MSS 1016; Uncat MS Vault 719; Uncat MS Vault File; Uncat MS Vault 716; Uncat MS Vault 719; and Uncat MS Vault 824.

bioghist.head: Leonard Baskin
bioghist.p: Leonard Baskin (1922-2000) was an American sculptor, book illustrator, wood engraver, printmaker, graphic artist, writer, and teacher. Baskin founded the Gehenna Press, a pioneering American fine art press, while a student at Yale University in 1942. The Gehenna Press continued in operation until Baskin's passing in 2000. Leonard Baskin was married to nature writer Esther Baskin until her death in 1973. His second wife was the political activist, book dealer, and collector Lisa Unger Baskin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection consists of business correspondence, personal correspondence, photographs, drawings, woodcut prints, artists' books, writings, clippings, page proofs, exhibition materials, printed materials, and other papers documenting the life and work of Leonard Baskin and the operations of the Gehenna Press. Among the works documented in the collection is The Defense of Gracchus Babeuf before the High Court of Vendôme, by Gracchus Babeuf , translated by John Anthony Scott and illustrated by Thomas Cornell (Gehenna Press, 1964). The collection also includes correspondence and other papers by or related to Esther Baskin.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Leonard Baskin and Lisa Unger Baskin, 1964, 1991, 1994, and 2011.
8 September 1994 acquisition: Gift of Louis L. Martz, 1994.
September 1995 acquisition: Gift of Hosea Baskin and Sarah Baskin, 1995.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call numbers: Uncat MSS 1016 and Uncat MS Vault 719.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This acquisition consists of typescripts, proofs, exhibition materials, correspondence, and other papers related to the 1964 Gehenna Press edition of The Defense of Gracchus Babeuf Before the High Cour of Vendôme, by Gracchus Babeuf (1760-1797) .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this acquisition are roughly in the order in which they were received.

did.unittitle: The Defense of Gracchus Babeuf Before the High Court of Vendôme, by Gracchus Babeuf (1760-1797)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains corrected typescript and photocopied drafts of the 1964 Gehenna Press edition, illustrated by Thomas Cornell and edited and translated by John Anthony Scott. Also contains typed research citation slips.

did.unittitle: The Defense of Gracchus Babeuf Before the High Court of Vendôme, by Gracchus Babeuf (1760-1797)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: Yale Library invitations regarding a topographical event for the book's publication; Yale Library exhibition cards from the reception; prospectus material for the book; corrected drafts and proofs; clippings; typescript of "The History of the Gehenna Press Edition of the Defense of Gracchus Babeuf;" Copyflo reproductions of material related to Gracchus Babeuf; "Correspondance de Babeuf avec l'Academie d'Arras (1785-1788)." Marcel Reinhard; Copyflo of "Historie de Gracchus Babeuf et du Babouvisme," Vol. II, Pages 1-322; an empty library case The Defense of Gracchus Babeuf material; and handwritten notes.

did.unittitle: The Defense of Gracchus Babeuf Before the High Court of Vendôme, by Gracchus Babeuf (1760-1797)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: Babeuf exhibition materials, including corrected typescripts and handwritten notes; correspondence from Thomas Cornell, Sidney Kaplan, John Anthony Scott, Samuel Bernstein, Leonard Baskin and others; a woodblock from The Defense of Gracchus Babeuf book; and microfilm of "Le Tribun du Peuple."

did.unittitle: Correspondence, printed material, photographs, artwork, and other papers by or relating to Leonard Baskin and the Gehenna Press

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Leonard Baskin 75th Birthday invitations; Franklin Delano Roosevelt Memorial material; "Native Americans: The Second Series" exhibition material; Gehenna's Fiftieth Anniversary material; Giambologna exhibition material; Woodrow Wilson Center correspondence; Salander-O'Reilly Galleries material; a House of Blues art guide; fliers for a Leonard Baskin one-man show at Bishop Gallery, Scotsdale, Arizona; Massachusetts Merit System Reporter, vol. 10, Winter 1997; and The New Collection of Modern Religious Art in the Vatican Museums booklet (1973).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until April 22, 2026. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
